highlights.text: Again - my lean was they could.

My general lean is we can fill this structure out, perhaps trap it, then head to 6846, 6873, 6893, then 6903.” By noon yesterday, we hit 6873 and then we spent today largely consolidating/flagging again beneath 6873.
Back on Monday March 30th, something important happened in ES: It put in my core setup - the Failed Breakdown.
As I frequently discuss all major rallies in ES start on Failed Breakdowns, because Failed Breakdowns are how institutions accumulate .

We saw it Monday March 30th as stated and this was a major accumulation event as institutions prepped for what ended up being a 480 point leg up - as always, we followed them : ES plunged to 6353 Monday March 30th in evening going elevator down .
An alternative is to wait for it to flush and recover (perhaps to 6353) for a Failed Breakdown.” From there, we were off to the races, getting to ~6662 by April 1st.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow
Targets would be 6677, 6691, 6716, then 6742” By the close Tuesday, we hit 6663 range resistance, and broke out viciously to 6850 by Wednesday morning, driven by Iran headlines.

post_title: SPX Puts In Biggest Green Week Since June 2025. Will It Run Next Week? April 13 Plan
